name: Antigua Villa de Moya — circular de GRAVEL desde Landete
name_original: Antigua Villa de Moya — circular de gravel desde Landete
region_intro: Touring cycling around Landete features routes that traverse varied terrain, including gravel paths and undulating landscapes with significant elevation changes. The region is characterized by historical towns, open roads, and viewpoints, offering a mix of cultural and natural exploration. Routes often include climbs to elevated areas, providing panoramic vistas. Best touring cycling routes around Landete The most popular touring cycling route is Antigua Villa de Moya — circular de GRAVEL desde Landete, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route features gravel sections and explores the area around the ancient town of Moya. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Arcos de las Salinas – Beautiful straight road out of Aras de Los Olmos loop from Santa Cruz de Moya, a difficult 52.8 miles (84.9 km) path. This route includes long straight road sections and significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Landete – Monasterio de Tejeda loop from Landete, a 27.8 miles (44.7 km) trail leading through the local landscape towards the Monasterio de Tejeda, often completed in about 2 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Landete is defined by historical sites, challenging climbs, and varied road surfaces including gravel. The network offers options for both easy rides and more demanding routes with substantial elevation. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Landete's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How challenging are the touring cycling routes in Landete?
A: Touring cycling around Landete offers a mix of challenges. While there is one easy route, the majority of the 9 available routes are rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation changes and varied terrain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31503378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcos de las Salinas – Beautiful straight road out of Aras de Los Olmos loop from Santa Cruz de Moya&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 52.8-mile path with over 2000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there easy routes for beginners or families in Landete?
A: Yes, there is one easy touring cycling route suitable for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086488974/antigua-villa-de-moya-circular-de-gravel-desde-landete" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antigua Villa de Moya — circular de GRAVEL desde Landete&lt;/a&gt; is a 10.6-mile trail that takes about 1 hour 21 minutes to complete, featuring gravel sections and exploring the area around the ancient town of Moya.

Q: Are there options for long-distance touring cycling in Landete?
A: Yes, Landete offers several options for longer touring cycling adventures. Many routes are rated as difficult and cover substantial distances. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dirt road to Moya – Old Town of Moya loop from Los Huertos&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 34.1-mile route that takes over 3.5 hours to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Landete?
A: The Landete region experiences hot, dry summers and cooler winters. The best times for touring cycling are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for longer rides. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available around Landete?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Landete are designed as circular loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landete – Monasterio de Tejeda loop from Landete&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.8-mile trail that takes you through the local landscape and back to Landete.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Landete?
A: The touring cycling routes in Landete often pass by historical sites and offer scenic views. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5707485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Town of Moya&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5403982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Tejeda&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Higueruela&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in cultural and natural exploration opportunities.

Q: Are there routes with scenic viewpoints in Landete?
A: Absolutely. The undulating landscapes of Landete mean many routes include climbs to elevated areas, providing panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506221" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sierra Viewpoint – View from Peña Roya loop from Talayuelas&lt;/a&gt; is a great option specifically highlighting scenic views, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the old town of Moya&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to refuel along the touring cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not always directly on every route, many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find places to refuel. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote sections, and plan your stops in advance using a map.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Landete?
A: Public transport options directly to the start points of specific touring cycling routes in Landete can be limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to the starting points, especially for routes that begin outside of the main town. Always check local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: Where can I park my car when cycling in Landete?
A: When starting a touring cycling route in Landete, you can typically find parking in the main town of Landete or in the smaller villages that serve as common starting points for the loops. Look for designated parking areas or suitable roadside spots, ensuring you do not obstruct local traffic or access.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Landete?
A: Touring cyclists using komoot often praise Landete for its varied terrain, which includes both gravel paths and open roads, and the challenging climbs that lead to rewarding panoramic views. The region's historical towns and cultural landmarks also add a unique dimension to the cycling experience.
tips: The ruins of the ancient Villa de Moya are one of the main archaeological sites of the Middle Ages. They date back to the 13th century and were declared a historical-artistic monument in 1982.
Fantastic dirt road, with views to the ruins of Moya on the hillside.
